BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Back-of-napkin model for Ward Taxi & Car Service LLC, run before the AHCA medical-transport licensing push on the early $4,000-per-van modification estimate, not the shop's current quote. Pre-Max Delano review.

CurrentState.Note: Rough estimate only. Insurance shown at the prior $3,200/mo. The MembershipDues row stands in for the two hotel contracts plus the Palmetto Health shuttle, InstructorPay_Raj stands in for the part-time driver payroll run, AaronDrawFromNet is Ashley's owner draw, and Rent is the fleet yard rent. Placeholder keys carried over from the template spreadsheet Ashley reused. Revisit with Max Delano once 2025 actuals are closed.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Two-van AHCA conversion at the early $4,000 per van estimate, $8,000 total, break-even about 14 months
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Total conversion outlay $8,000 divided by incremental net of roughly $571 per van per month across two vans reaches break-even at about 14 months. STALE: uses the $4,000 per van estimate, not the shop's current quote.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Stale. The shop's current quote is higher, so recompute before quoting the timeline.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Fleet insurance renewal jumps roughly 10% on the November 1 Coral Gables policy to $3,520/mo
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Still fine. Absorbed within the owner draw without touching reserves.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Driver payroll, fuel and fleet insurance are the three levers. Reserves can fund the AHCA van conversions without new debt. The van-conversion break-even here uses the stale $4,000 per van figure and must be recomputed off the shop's current quote.
